01BBF041: Planting a Garden isn't all sunshine and roses when Zombies are on the loose!  Plant a Garden and defend it from waves of invading Zombies with up to three of your online friends. You can also set up a private match to go it alone and rely on your Potted Plants to help stop the incoming Horde.\n\nTo get started, choose your Plant character and a Garden location, and then work to keep your Garden from being trampled into dust by the Zombies!
01BBF042: If the Zombies have sucked all the life out of your health bar, there's a limited time window during which your teammates can revive you. If all players are down or the Garden's master health bar completely drains, you lose — at least until it's time to sow more seeds and seek your revenge in a new match!

Thanks for joining Crazy Dave and Dr. Zomboss for the PvZ GW2 TRIAL.

Before jumping in, we'd like to point out that you have access to the full PvZ GW2 game for 10-hours starting right now.

Also all progress you make will carry-over, should you decide to upgrade to the full game once the trial is over...and to be honest, who wouldn't? It's amazing!

1280D8EB:
Hero Showcase lets you try out two Heroes all week! This week only get charged with Power Cactus and Rugby Star!

POWER CACTUS:
- Strengths: Long range specialist – deals damage to groups of enemies from a safe distance. Zombies won't know what hit them!
- Weakness: Low health – don't let Scientists and Pirates come too close and always watch your back!  
- Try this character because: Projectiles generate an Electric Arc that jumps between the enemy you hit and enemies around him.

RUGBY STAR: 
- Strength: Large amount of hit points – let them hit you while dealing constant damage.
- Weakness: Movement speed while aiming or firing - if spotted, can be taken down by any Cacti or Rose character.
- Try this character because: Primary weapon fires explosive rugby balls, why be accurate when you can do area damage?

If you get three of a kind you'll trigger a Super Boss Wave, so ready your petals for a challenge.

146A23E8: Plants and Zombies square off in an epic turf war! Zombies attack Gardens and turn them into Graveyards, while Plants defend their Gardens from a withered fate by fighting off the undead invaders.\n\nSee how many Gardens the Zombies can capture before the time expires. Each time they capture a Garden, the timer resets. Each team earns Coins based on how many Gardens they were able to capture or defend. Get them all for an Epic win!

14D6018C:
Hero Showcase lets you try out two Heroes all week! This week only play Agent Pea and Centurion!

AGENT PEA:
- Strengths: Mobility of the Peashooter with the accuracy of a Cacti. Great at long and close range encounters. 
- Weakness: Missing headshots reduce the effectiveness of this character, so be precise!
- Try this character because: He's fast, mobile and with the right aim, Agent Pea can tip the battle in your favor. 

CENTURION: 
- Strength: Accurate shot with medium damage and a burn effect to hurt enemies even when you need to reload.
- Weakness: Single Fire crossbow means he can't rush into fights alone, must stick with allies. 
- Try this character because: Love supporting your team and getting the win? Centurion is the right guy for the job.

Toxic Citron is leading the charge of elite plants to destroy Zomboss's Ultra-Zombot 6000* factory and its guardian, the crunchy caped-crusader Breakfast Brains.
* With rechargeable overshield
 
What's new in the game:
* New map, Zombopolis
* New characters, Toxic Citron and Breakfast Brainz
* 1,200 new customization items
* Character balancing
* Gameplay improvements 

See the blog for more info: http://www.pvzgw2.com/news/trouble-in-zombopolis

Hero Showcase lets you try out two Heroes all week! This week only play BBQ Corn and Cosmic Brainz!

BBQ CORN:
- Strengths: Fast attacker, lots of mobility, high rate of fire, and projectiles hot enough to burn zombies.
- Weakness: Indoor plants as underground and interior spaces block his Butter Barrage.
- Try this character because: Deadliest corn around, can fight well alone, and is unmatched when playing as a team.

COSMIC BRAIN: 
- Strength: Can snipe targets galaxies away and deliver cosmic punches capable of warping plants out of this world.
- Weakness: Slow punch rate means Cosmic Brains needs the help of his allies to stay alive in big fights.
- Try this character because: Chargeable Punches! Send plants to a new dimension with a fully charged up punch.

198052CE:
Hero Showcase lets you try out two Heroes all week! This week only play Hot Rod Chomper and Sanitation Expert!

HOT ROD CHOMPER:
- Strengths: Faster variant of the regular Chomper - get in, chomp, get out.
- Weakness: No range attacks, needs to engage the enemies head on, so watch out for snipers.
- Try this character because: Like getting to the fight, and winning it, really fast? Hot Rod is your plant.

SANITATION EXPERT: 
- Strength: Fires Stinky trash balls that explode upon impact and deal Toxic damage over time. Not your friendly neighborhood Garbage man.
- Weakness:  Snipers, this guy is a big target for sharpshooting enemies, stay mobile and behind cover.
- Try this character because: Exploding balls of toxic garbage! Area damage means more throwing, less aiming!

You puny L.E.A.F. sprouts think you can take down my factory! You haven't considered the genius of Zomboss – or the importance of breakfast. But waiting for you in Zombopolis is proof of both. Breakfast Brainz! 

Level up for the battle in Zombopolis with Max XP through June 5.

Breakfast Brainz will take down servers from Tuesday, May 31st at 7:00 AM PDT (2:00 PM GMT) and will be back up at 11:00 AM PDT (6:00 PM GMT) to prepare for Zomboss' big reveal.

2109A53A: \n\nNow that we have your attention...  You have less than 48 hours left to unlock the featured Hero Showcase characters. If you really like one of the characters, you can head to the Character Select or Sticker Shop and use coins to unlock it. That way you can play with the character even after the Hero Showcase expires. Which, in case we didn't mention it before, is in less than 48 hours.

2226B933: Hold down jump to soar to new heights.
2226B934: Use the Super Ultra Ball ability while in the air, you will stop long enough to aim.
2226B935: Use Heroic Kick while in the air and aiming at the enemy to strike from above.
2226B936: Heroic Kick is great for fast travel or getting onto hard to reach places.
2226B937: Super Brainz is a melee character, get in close to the enemy to maximize your damage.
2226B938: Make sure you always stretch before a fight!
2226B939: If there's a mirror and you're playing Super Brainz, you are obligated to stop and flex.
2226B93A: You take far less damage when using Turbo Twister, use it to get out of tight situations.
2226B93B: Turbo Twister is great against groups of weeds.
2226B93C: Cosmic Brainz punches so hard he sends enemies into a black hole where they are unrevivable!
2226B954: Captain Deadbeard spent the last 500 years adrift on a piece of wood.  He may be a little "off".
2226B955: He may have shaky hands but Captain Deadbeard can play the flute like nobody's business
2226B956: His primary fire is short range but hold down aim to switch to ranged attack mode.
2226B957: There are so many cool things you can do with a barrel!  You can sit in it… you can… sit in it!
2226B958: Use a Barrel Blast when you see an enemy coming but they don't see you, it's a great disguise!
2226B959: When in a barrel you get a temporary speed boost when you light the fuse, make it count!
2226B95A: If Captain Deadbeard takes damage while flying the parrot he gets knocked out of parrot control.
2226B95B: Cannon Rodeo is great for bombarding strategic locations.. Like gardens!
2226B95C: Don't let them see you activate Cannon Rodeo, you're a target.  Activate it when nobody can see you.
2226B95D: If you cancel out of Cannon Rodeo without firing a shot you don't lose the cooldown on that ability.
2226B975: Scurvy Scattershot and Spyglass Shot share the same ammo pool.  The Scattershot takes up more ammo.
2226B976: Be Careful!  Gravity Grenade may lift enemies into place, but they can still shoot back.
2226B977: Imps have very low health and need to jump around and move constantly to stay in the fight.
2226B978: Be aware of opponents that do splash damage when playing as the Imp, they are your worst enemy.
2226B979: Just because you can call in a Z-Mech doesn't mean you should.  Timing is everything!
2226B97A: Impkata is best used when surrounded by enemies!  It's also a great party trick!
2226B97B: Gravity grenade a citron before they can E.M.Peach you, if you do this you will almost always win.
2226B97C: Beware the Citron's E.M.Peach if you're in a Z-Mech, it will stun you for longer.
2226B97D: Keep constant watch on your health when in a Z-Mech, eject when it gets low or you go down with the Mech.
2226B97E: Eject from the Z-Mech when in a crowd to set off its self destruct.
2226B996: Imps can double jump by using their Butt Boosters.  Use this to evade enemy fire, jump over cliffs and just look cool.
2226B997: What Imps lack in health they make up for with speed and firepower.
2226B998: Using double jump while holding different movement directions can make the imp perform special flips.
2226B999: Citron is a bounty hunter orange from the future!
2226B99A: The E.M.Peach is a Z-Mech's worst nightmare, it can stun lock them for a long time.
2226B99B: Use your Peel Shield early when taking damage, it can be the difference maker.
2226B99C: Don't get flanked when using your Peel Shield and always face the enemy.
2226B99D: When in Citron Ball form you're still dangerous.  Use Spin Dash ability to charge up and launch yourself at the enemy.
2226B99E: If it feels like you're losing the fight jump into Ball Form and roll out of there!
2226B99F: Use the Hyper Ball ability when in Ball Form to get to strategic locations first.
2226B9B7: Each Citron character has unique tattoos to unlock, these tattoos also change how you look in ball form.
2226B9B8: The Citron may be slow in Assault Form, but he can hold ground better than any other Plant.
2226B9B9: Try not to overheat your Orange Beam or you'll be vulnerable for a few seconds.
2226B9BA: Rose had a vision of the future and it was FULL of Zombies!  She's come forward in time to lend a hand.
2226B9BB: Rose attacks her enemies with Magic Thistles, which home in on enemies in her sights and explode on impact.
2226B9BC: Close counts when playing Rose, try to keep the magic flowing towards the enemy.
2226B9BD: Time Snare is the ultimate way to open an engagement.  Trap your enemy so that you have time for more powerful spells.
2226B9BF: When vanquished Rose has an enchantment that turns her into a small long stem rose, that's how she can be revived.
2226B9C0: Arcane Enigma is both offensive and defensive.  It has so many practical applications!
2226B9D8: Use Arcane Enigma to jump in the middle of a group of enemies and use attack to take them down with arcane bursts.
2226B9D9: Rose is nearly invincible when in Arcane Enigma form, a perfect ability to get the upper hand or retreat from a bad spot.
2226B9DA: Look for Zombies clustered together.  With a well placed shot Rose can turn several Zombies into goats!
2226B9DB: Beware a goatified Zombie, they may just go on the offensive and try to ram you.  That would be embarrassing!
2226B9DC: Corn was fighting overseas during the events of Garden Warfare 1.  He's home now and ready to take back Suburbia!
2226B9DD: With his dual Cob Busters Kernel Corn is primed and ready for action.
2226B9DE: Kernel Corn prefers a "fast action, no retreat" kind of tactic, he's great at getting in on the enemies flanks.
2226B9DF: The Butter Barrage is very devastating but takes a while to call in, best to plan ahead or use it to clear areas.
2226B9E0: Look up when calling in the Butter Barrage, you'll get to see the Butterhawks in action.
2226B9E1: The Husk Hop is great for gaining a height advantage or leaping over the enemy and showering them with corn.
2226B9F9: The Shuck Shot is extremely damaging, but try not to miss!
2226B9FA: Try not to stay in one place for too long, Kernel Corn is best when on the move.
2226B9FB: Throw that hot potato and watch the butter rain down, it's an amazing sight.  Always makes us hungry!
2226B9FC: Sometimes it's best to use the Butter Barrage as an artillery shield to prevent enemies from getting close to an objective.
2226B9FD: You can't butter up the Kernel!
2226B9FE: Stay in the starting area to be safe, once you venture out all bets are off.
2226B9FF: Plants on one side, Zombies on the other.  What could possibly go wrong?
2226BA00: There are many cool areas and secrets within the Backyard Battleground.  Look around, but bring stars with you!
2226BA01: Earn stars by completing quests from the Quest Board.  Stars are used as a form of currency around the Backyard.
2226BA02: Quests in the Quest Board will refresh over time, active quests in your inventory don't go away unless you cancel them.
2226BA1A: Nobody knows who built the portals, but Crazy Dave and Dr. Zomboss have put them to good use.
2226BA1B: If you're ever in trouble, Plant a tree under your house.  What's the worst that could happen?
2226BA1C: Set your Backyard to allow friends to join so they can pop in to visit!  Friend permissions can be set in World Options.
2226BA1D: Join your friends with the push of a button through the World Options Station.
2226BA1E: The more you fight the enemy in the Backyard the more the battle will spiral out of control.
2226BA1F: Jump into the enemy base and see how long you can last!
2226BA20: Grow Pots and Build Bots! They are there to help!
2226BA21: Champions are rare golden enemies that have more health and drop treasure when vanquished.
2226BA22: Chompers can't swallow bosses, but it's a good way to disrupt their attacks and help the team.
2226BA23: Chili beans can be served with cheese.
2226BA3B: Complete quests from the quest board to earn stars and an XP multiplier!
2226BA3C: The XP multiplier will go down over time when you're not playing the game - so come back as often as you can to keep it high!
2226BA3D: Did you know, Crazy Dave is Crazy?
2226BA3E: Chompers can destroy a Zombie's shield by biting it!
2226BA3F: Don't let vampire Zombies hit you! They will heal themselves.
2226BA40: Stars have many benefits, use them wisely.
2226BA41: Don't trust fish with a star obsession!
2226BA42: Every Pot and Bot is different. Get to know them well.  Maybe take them to a movie?
2226BA43: In Ops, try staying in front of your defenses to start, then fall back when things get ugly.
2226BA44: Zombies love spicy food. They won't ignore a ripe chili bean.
2226BA5C: Weeds can't stand tackle decoys. They just don't like Zombie sporting equipment.  It stinks!
2226BA5D: No Winter Fest would be complete without a Gargle Bargle tournament.
2226BA5E: Zombies agree that yetis are not robots! These are the ramblings of a crazy person.
2226BA5F: The timeless ones are always watching. Be on your best behavior.
2226BA60: The Suburbia and Zomburbia divide stretches further than everything. Ever. Seriously it's like really far.
2226BA61: Plants are the first and only line of defense against Zombies.
2226BA62: A Pirate's hands are always clammy!
2226BA64: Zombies think the world is a ball that rotates under their feet when they run.  Errr... wait a second.
2226BD74: Crazy Dave REALLY does hate cats.
2226BD75: Have you ever seen a singularity up close?
2226BD76: Are you secretly a buttered piece of toast?
2226BD77: Want to try new characters in the backyard?  Use the Customization Booth or select Respawn from the Pause Menu.
2226BD78: Selecting Warp Home from the Pause Menu while in the Backyard Battleground will bring you back to home base.  Perfect for when you're utterly lost!
2226BD79: Legendary characters have legendary meters, fill them up to trigger their more powerful legendary mode.  Get vanquished however and you need to start again.
2226BD7A: Rose can summon portals in Herbal Assault which can help bring the team to the front line in a hurry.
2226BD7B: Bosses can't be stunned by Sonic Mines, Sonic Grenades or E.M.Peach abilities.
2226BD7C: Rose can't turn bosses into goats, they're simply too powerful!
2226BD7D: Kernel Corn's Butter Barrage is most effective with clear skies above.
2226BD95: Bosses can't be frozen in place by ice attacks, but can still be slowed down.

ID_HUB_MISSION_P_CITRON_04_NPC_IRONBALL_: Wow, am I glad to see you!
The name is Ironball.
I was chasing a bounty, but man he was a tricky one..
I messed up and ended up getting captured.
Now that you are here we can take him down as a team.
But you gotta bust me outta here first...
That Robo-guard over there has the key.
Hunk o' junk won't know what hit him!
Go get that key and get me out of here!
You found the key! Nice work.
Now lets go find that bounty.
I saw him heading towards the docks.
Let's roll out!
I know who to call next time I go fishing for shrimp.
SHRIMP KA-POW!
Now THAT was some prime time bounty hunting!

3790E69F:
Welcome to Plants vs Zombies Garden Warfare 2! We hope you enjoy playing the game as much as we've enjoyed making it!  There's tons of stuff to do and see, so take some time to explore the backyard battleground.  Remember, there's always something hiding around the next corner!  Ok that sounded a little creepy...  How about this...  There's always something ridiculously amazing around the next corner!

Sincerely, 
The development team
3790E6A0: \n\nCompleting quests from the Quest Board earns you pins, which in turn can increase your XP multiplier, letting you level up your characters faster. Keep in mind though that your multiplier will start to decrease if you haven't played Garden Warfare 2 in the last 24 hours.
3790E6A1: \n\nThe Turf Takeover playlist features the classic Gardens & Graveyards mode as well as the all new Herbal Assault mode.
3790E6A2: \n\nDid you know that whenever you gain a level for a character you also gain a player rank? Also when you hit level 100 you earn a new Rank Plate as well as a small celebration in your honor!
3790E6A3: \n\nOnce you've unlocked a Character Upgrade make sure to equip it to each character you want to play. Upgrades are unlocked by leveling up a character, they let you personalize your playstyle!
3790E6A4: \n\nYou can Change Abilities in the character customization menu once you've unlocked them. As an example Scientists can swap between New Heal Beam and Classic Heal Station.
3790E6A5: \n\nThere are certain places where you simply shouldn't pan for gold.
3790E6A6: \n\nThe Hero Showcase allows you to try new characters you haven't unlocked.  Keep in mind you can only play them for a limited time.  The cool thing is you still get to keep all progress and stats you earn with those characters when you unlock them.
3790E6A7: \n\nWhen you hit level 10 make sure to visit the stats room to promote your character. Promoting a character will grant them with a new title and lets you unlock more upgrades and rewards!
3790E6A8: \n\nSunflowers and Scientists aren't just great for keeping your allies health high. Did you know that these characters revive allies to full health instead of the standard half health?
3790E6C0: \n\nOps Tip: Did you know when playing ops, using a Team Retry will replenish your Self Revives? Your team only has one Retry per game so use it wisely!
3790E6C1: \n\nWould you know if you ate a perfect sandwich?  Or would you just carry on with your life, oblivious to the truth.
3790E6C2: \n\nCitron Tip: Did you know Citrons E.M.Peach lasts longer vs Imp Mechs and does more damage!
3790E6C3: \n\nTeam Vanquish Tip: In Team Vanquish if you revive an ally it takes score away from the other team. Reviving is also a great way to make new friends!
3790E6C4: \n\nTurf Takeover: Engineers and Roses can build teleporters. These help get everyone on your team get into the action quicker and are essential to attacking the bases.
3790E6C5: \n\nKeep a fresh pair of socks in your pocket, who knows when you may need them!
3790E6C6: \n\nMinion Tip: Flag Zombies and Flag Weeds give a defense boost too nearby allies Stock up at the Sticker Shop before you jump into Turf Takeover!
3790E6C7: \n\nRose Tip: Did you know that you can Goatify an Imp right as he is calling in his Mech? You can also Goatify more than one Zombie at the same time if they're beside each other!
3790E6C8: \n\nGnome Bomb: Did you know that picking up the Gnome Bomb will cancel your abilities? When holding a gnome move quickly or it will blow up!
3790E6C9: \n\nCaptain Deadbeard Tip: Did you know that you take less damage while hiding in your barrel. Lighting the fuse will let you move faster. Sneaky Swashbucklers hide around corners.
3790E6E1: \n\nDid you know that capture points move faster with more people in the zone? Capture points with allies to maximize your effectiveness.
3790E6E2: \n\nTry not to talk to fish unless they talk to you first, some people may think you're crazy.
3790E6E3: \n\nDid you know that in Ops mode enchanted gardens or graveyards may appear at the start of the game? They cost a star to build but shower you with extra coins after each wave is completed!
3790E6E4: \n\nKernel Corn Tip: His Butter Barrage ability is great at clearing out capture points. Add Chives and Salt for maximum effect.
3790E6E5: \n\nIf you have really smelly feet quickly check in the mirror, you may just be a Zombie!
3790E6E6: \n\nPeashooter Tip: Peashooters have great splash damage and can take out low health enemies like an Imp quickly!
3790E6E7: \n\nSoldier Tip: The ZPG is a great counter to rooted Peashooters and Sunflowers.
3790E6E8: \n\nIf you feel that you may in fact be a buttered piece of toast we recommend seeing a doctor.
3790E6E9: \n\nEngineer Tip: Did you know Sonic Grenades can knock burrowed Chompers out of the ground?  He is the Zombies best defender against Chompers.  Also, Chomper's can't chomp an engineer when he's on his jackhammer.  They eat the jackhammer instead!  Ouch!
3790E6EA: \n\nAll-Star Tip: Did you know weeds can't resist attacking a Tackle Dummy, line them up in chokepoints for the best defense. Deploying the shield to block an attack could save your life.
3790E702: \n\nCactus Tip: The Garlic Drones Corn Strike is highly effective at taking out zombie teleporters and clearing out capture points.
3790E703: \n\nIf you're having trouble getting near the objective try flanking, if you can get in behind the enemy you can more easily take them out.
3790E704: \n\nIf you're not sure what it is, don't be afraid to ask.  It may be valuable!  It may also just be a very small rock.

3859C711:
In PvZ Garden Warfare 2, almost everything you do gives you XP. To earn XP faster, power up your XP multiplier by completing quests from the Quest Board! The higher your multiplier, the faster you can level up. Some quests also give you stars, which you can use to open secret chests (shhh!). 

Pro Tip: Completing quests from the Quest Board is the only way to increase your XP multiplier in PvZ GW2. Log in every day to keep yours at maxed out.

Due a temporal rift that appeared when a Dr. Zomboss world domination experiment didn't go as planned, you can now try 4 new characters for the next 5 days! Use them as much as you want to fully experience their lawn-inspiring abilities. Head to the Mailbox each week for a Hero Showcase, and find one fresh plant and one freshly-stinky zombie to play with in the full game.

Available now, for 5 days: 
• Druid Rose
• BBQ Corn 
• Lil' Drake 
• Toxic Brains

64EC50A6: Note: Garden Ops supports one to four players, and the number of Zombies you fight will adjust according to how many people are playing.
ID_M_WEP_DESC_PIRATEBARREL_BLING: Yo ho ho, it's a barrel made of shiny treasure! Protect yourself from plants in style, and blast them away in a glittery explosion!
ID_ZOMBOPOLIS_ATTACK_MINIGAME1: Attack ZOMBOT before they regenerate his shield!
655E5C77: The Zombies have crash landed in the desert, near a top secret Plant research facility under the golf course. If the Plants don't stop them, they'll ruin everything!
ID_HUB_MISSION_P_CORN_F_GR_01: You're a heck of a fighter!
ID_HUB_MISSION_P_CORN_F_GR_02: I am proud to have served with you, and will give you a well earned commendation.  And a COB Badge of Honor!
ID_H_HUNT_CU4_DAVE_YETIDANCE06: Ice Cream is supposed\nto be fun.
ID_H_OPTION_HUD_VISIBLITY_HELP: Determines what HUD UI to display
65AF1448: Sharper metal flakes cause more damage.
65E22483: There's power in numbers. Stick close to teammates!
65E22484: You're able to shoot while reviving a teammate. Provide your own cover fire!
65E22485: Injured characters will only auto-regenerate to half health—look for healing characters to restore the rest.
65E22486: Taking damage will interrupt health regeneration; best to keep a safe distance.
65E22487: Keep a tight defense when protecting Gardens. Make the Zombies fight you where you're strongest.
65E22488: Try different combinations of Potted Plants to support your play style.
65E22489: Plant a Heal Flower if your team has too few Sunflowers. A little healing can go a long way!
65E2248A: Root in a bad spot? Don't shoot! Just unroot and your ability won't enter cooldown.
65E2248B: When enemies are moving, try to aim for their feet - misses will still do splash damage.
65E224A3: Use Hyper to jump around your enemies quickly, shooting at them from above while making yourself hard to hit.
65E224A4: Having higher ground allows you to take full advantage of the Pea Cannon's splash damage.
65E224A5: Zombies are attracted to the Chili Bean, so use it to clear out large groups!
65E224A6: You can throw your Chili Bean further if you run and jump before throwing.
65E224A7: Save your Pea Gatling for longer range targets—and watch for ZPGs!
65E224A8: Keep one eye on the sky and clear the rooftops of pesky Soldier Zombies.
65E224A9: Use Hyper to race to strategic positions and hold the ground for the rest of the team.
65E224AA: Getting chased? Throw a Chili Bean against the wall in front of you so it bounces back and takes out your pursuer!
65E224AB: The Peashooter gets critical hits for landing a pea directly on their opponent.  Why?  Because peas are dangerous!
65E224AC: The Peashooter is at his best when he stays mobile during a fight.
65E224C4: When you get in over your head, Hyper out and find a safe spot to regenerate your health.
65E224C5: In Gardens & Graveyards, it's always a race to the Teleporter location between Peashooters and Engineer Zombies. Try to be 1st.
65E224C6: A Sunflower healing a Chomper or Citron can make for a powerful close quarters duo.
65E224C7: Sunflowers are able to revive their teammates much more quickly than other Plants.
65E224C8: The Sunflower has the fastest normal movement speed of all characters. That's pretty helpful for tracking down and healing teammates!
65E224C9: Although the Sunflower's attack is not as powerful as others, it's accurate and easy to control. Aim for the head to do more damage!
65E224CA: The intensity of the Sunbeam ability will alert the entire Zombie team to your location - so be ready to uproot at a moment's notice.
65E224CB: Drop your Heal Flower and fight near it to keep yourself in a constant state of healing.
65E224CC: Sunflowers can heal each other, so establish a 2-way Heal Beam and keep each other alive throughout the fight!
65E224CD: Don't forget: Potted Plants can be healed too. Keep them happy and healthy!
65E224E5: Leave Potato Mines around the Garden to take out Browncoats, and unsuspecting Zombie players.
65E224E6: Tallnuts can be used for cover, but they can also be used to slow down advancing Browncoats.
65E224E7: Use Tallnuts as steps to access areas you can't quite jump up to.
65E224E8: Use the Garlic Drone's Corn Strike ability to clear out hordes of Browncoats.
65E224E9: When flying the Garlic Drone, always keep moving. Your low health makes it easy to be taken out by one well-placed hit.
65E224EA: Corn Strikes are great for taking out Zombie Teleporters and Healing Stations.
65E224EB: Scientist about to Warp towards you? Drop a Potato Mine while backing up and ruin his day.
65E224EC: Try placing Potato Mines directly around corners or at the top of stairs. Most players won't have time to react, and then SPUDOW!
65E224ED: The Cactus is the best ranged fighter on the Plant team. Get to a safe distance, then use those Tallnuts and Potato Mines to stay there!
65E224EE: Potato Mines are great at covering your flanks and rear when sniping from a set position.
65E22506: Goop your target to ensure they can't use their abilities or get away.
65E22507: The best Chomper is a sneaky Chomper.  Hide behind cover and let your enemy come to you before attacking.
65E22508: In Gardens & Graveyards, Chompers are great at defending the Gardens.  Make the Zombies think twice about getting close!
65E22509: Chompers are helpless for a short time after swallowing a victim.  Try to attack Zombies that stray from the group.
65E2250A: The Engineer's Sonic Grenades are the biggest threat to a Chomper.  Chomp them first, when possible, but don't let them see you coming.
65E2250B: Plant your Spikeweeds in grassy, naturally camouflaging areas, then sit back and wait for easy prey.
65E2250C: A Chomper's Burrow stamina meter goes down slower when they don't move - perfect for an ambush.
65E2250D: Your Burrow can be used to approach the enemy or to retreat safely – use it wisely.
65E2250E: Even when the enemy is out of range, Gooping them can help your teammates get a Vanquish and get you an Assist.
65E2250F: A Gooped Zombie turns slower than normal - ideal for getting behind them for a Chomp.
65E22527: Use your brainz!  Individual Zombies don't stand much of a chance - attack the Plants as a group.
65E22528: Keep an eye out for the Scientist's Healing Station.
65E22529: Spawn Browncoats in Gardens & Graveyards to overwhelm the Plant Garden.
65E2252A: One out of ten Zombies agree, the Zombies is smarter than the Plants.
65E2252B: Only the brightest Zombies graduated from Zomboss Academy to join the fight for Brainz.
65E2252C: Take out rooted Plants with the ZPG. A direct hit will finish them off every time.
65E2252D: Use your Rocket Jump to get up on rooftops. It gives a good vantage point and keeps you safe from prowling Chompers.
65E2252E: As a Soldier Zombie, your job is to keep the Peashooters off the rooftops and keep the skies clear of Garlic Drones.
65E2252F: A well-placed Zombie Stink Cloud can temporarily blind the Plant team's turrets, allowing your team to advance.
65E22530: Deploy your Zombie Stink Cloud at the Garden in Gardens & Graveyards to make it hard for the Plants to defend.
65E22548: The Z1 Assault Blaster is great for laying down covering fire. Provide support from nearby rooftops to help your teammates advance.
65E22549: Use your Rocket Jump to quickly evade approaching Chompers.
65E2254A: Want to feel like a superhero? Throw down a Zombie Stink Cloud at your feet, Rocket Jump into the air, and vanquish an enemy with your ZPG before you land.
65E2254B: The Scientist's job is to heal his fellow Zombies - Deploy your Heal Station in areas where your team can see and use it.
65E2254C: Leave Sticky Explody Balls around corners when retreating. Your Plant pursuers will never know what hit them!
65E2254D: The Scientist's weapons are extremely powerful at point-blank range - close distances to your enemies by using Warp.
65E2254E: Use one Warp to get into a close range fight, but save the other for getting out of harm's way.  Heal, Repeat.
65E2254F: The Scientist can revive teammates faster than any other Zombie.
65E22550: Use the Heal Beam of Science to keep fellow Zombies healed and in the fight.
65E22551: Use Warp as a quick and effective way to evade nearby Chompers.
65E22569: Try to Warp past Plants and attack them from behind.
65E2256A: It's a long run between bases in Gardens & Graveyards.  Build Teleporters to help your team advance more quickly.
65E2256B: Build and upgrade Turrets near Teleporters and objectives to keep the Plants at bay.
65E2256C: Your Sonic Grenade stuns and slows opponents, making for an easy vanquish.
65E2256D: Having trouble hitting enemies head-on? Aim for their feet! Misses will still do splash damage.
65E2256E: Any direct hit with the Concrete Launcher is a critical hit.
65E2256F: Make yourself a difficult target by using your Jackhammer to sprint around opponents.
65E22570: While riding your Jackhammer, Chompers are unable to swallow you. Use this to your advantage!
65E22571: Use the Zombot Drone to scout ahead, and to drop Cone Strikes on groups of Plant defenders.
65E22572: While powerful, the Zombot Drone isn't very durable. Never stop moving!
65E2258A: The All-Star's cannon is good at suppressing enemies at long range and is extremely damaging up close.
65E2258B: Use the Sprint Tackle to finish off weakened foes, or to dodge into cover!
65E2258C: Dummy Shields can be used to provide cover while healing at Zombie Heal Stations, or while reviving an ally.
65E2258D: Use the Sprint Tackle to evade incoming Chomper attacks.
65E2258E: The All-Star has the highest health of all Zombies, perfect for capturing Plant-controlled Gardens.
65E2258F: Punt Imps into groups of Plants to maximize their damage.
65E22590: Well placed Tackle Dummies are essential to helping your team capture Plant-controlled Gardens.
65E22591: Helping teammates take down the opposition can earn you assists. Every little bit helps!
65E22592: Another player can become your Nemesis by vanquishing you multiple times.
65E22593: You can earn extra Coins by vanquishing an enemy that recently KO'd one of your teammates.
65E225AB: Vanquish an enemy before they take out your teammate to earn credit for saving an ally.
65E225AC: In Team Vanquish Mode, reviving a fallen teammate deducts a point from your opponents' score!
65E225AD: In Ops Mode, your pool of Self-Revives is limited per game. Don't waste 'em!
65E225AE: In Ops Mode, your pool of Team Retries is limited and shared across the entire team. Use them wisely!
65E225AF: Watch out! The Gargantuar & Yeti are both immune to ice elemental effects.
65E225B0: The Yeti can freeze you quickly if you get too close. Avoid fighting him at close range!
65E225B1: Chomp the Disco Zombie from below to stop his Whirlwind attack!
65E225B2: The Disco Zombie can't be damaged while using his Whirlwind attack. Save your ammo!
65E225B3: When the Giga-Gargantuar's around, you'd better head for cover. His attacks pack a punch!
65E225B4: Super Brainz likes doing squats as much as he likes throwing punches.

6B043030: An amazing new discovery: Smashing the treasure into broken bits has improved his damage.

6B7559C4: Grown from the most toxic Plant food, the Toxic Pea has a decreased reload time.
6B85AF26: My shop is open, and I've got some brand new items!
6B85AF27: If you've got the coins, Why don't you take a look?

Friends can now join you in PvZ Garden Warfare 2 for free during a limited-time 10-hour trial! Together you can:

• Explore the Backyard Battleground
• Play Flag of Power mode
• Enter Garden or Graveyard Ops modes (with up to 3 friends)
• Team up for multiplayer
• Show off your fancy-plants customization

Playing with friends in PvZ GW2 has never been easier.  Just use the World Options station to invite or join your friends.

This trial runs through May 10th, 10am (17:00 UTC)

ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_02: Usually we wouldn't be talking to each other, but I'm not like the other gnomes.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_03: I'm a happy gnome that helps others find a pot of gold.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_04: If you want to find the hidden gold, you need to wear a special hat.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_05: The hat is inside that chest, but you need to find the key first.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_06: Are you ready to pull your socks up and find the key?
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_07: You're back! Are you ready to find some gold?
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_08: The only way to find the gold is to wear the Leprechaun Hat.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_09: It will give you special vision. So make sure you always put it on before searching for the dig site.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_10: Check back every day while I am still here if you want to find more gold.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_12: Still want more gold don't you?
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_13: Don't forget to wear that Leprechaun Hat before you search.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_15: Look who's back for more gold.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_16: Do you want to start now?
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_17: It is my last day here today.
ID_HUB_CU5_MISSION_N_LUCKOZOMBIE_18: Are you ready to find the last bit of gold?

ID_HUB_MISSION_Z_SUPERBRAINZ_03_NPC_COZM: TA-DA! It's COZMIC BRAINZ, multiversal protector of AWESOME!

The LEAGUE OF AWESOME reunion happens now!

When you get a customization item in a Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare pack, you keep that item as long as you keep playing Plants vs. Zombies Warfare!  Simple as that. Don't let the opposition make a meal of you! As you take damage, the screen reddens to indicate that you're injured. Your health bar will also deplete and your health number will decrease.

For an easy way to check on your status, take a look at the heart symbol in the lower left corner of the screen — when it starts beating hard and becomes heavily bandaged, you'll know you need to heal up fast!

Move yourself away from direct combat to regenerate up to half of your overall health, or look for a Sunflower or Zombie Healing Station to regain health.

CDB07E68: Are you ready to make it rain ammunition? In Boss Mode, command the battlefield from the skies as Crazy Dave in his flying RV or Dr. Zomboss in his ZomBlimp. From your aerial perspective, you can spot enemy targets, deploy airstrikes, and revive and heal your teammates on the ground. To get started, select BOSS MODE from the character spawn screen. This is available for every game mode in Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare, but only one player per team can play Boss Mode at any given time. Once you're ready to unleash your bossy side, use your abilities to aid your team and be the best commander Suburbia has ever seen. After using abilities, collect resources to recharge them—that means catching Sun Drops as Crazy Dave or eating Brainz as Dr. Zomboss.

D17585B0: When you purchase a Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare pack, you receive certain items depending upon what type of pack you purchase.  Packs may include a combination of consumable items, customization items, character pieces and character upgrades.  Character pieces and upgrades, however, will never show up in a Reinforcement Pack or a Super Duper Pack.  In addition, each item is classified as Common, Uncommon, Rare or Super Rare.  Some Rare and Super Rare items provide game advantages, such as faster reload times, damage increases and new characters and weapons.

ID_BETA_CONTENT_DISCLAIMER: This game is in a pre-release stage of development, as a result, some parts of the game might not function as expected. During your time in the Plants vs Zombies Garden Warfare 2 Multiplayer Beta you may encounter issues such as bugs, server lag and downtime. We're working on identifying and addressing these issues, to ensure that we provide the best experience possible when the full game will be launched. However we do not commit to providing customer support for the Plants vs Zombies Garden Warfare 2 Multiplayer Beta as this is a pre-release game. Progress made during the Beta will NOT carry forward into the full game. Now that we've taken care of that...  Enjoy the Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare 2 Multiplayer Beta.\n

D599AEAD:
Welcome to Zomburbia! There are 14 character classes in PvZ Garden Warfare 2, each with unique abilities and play styles. Give your characters a quick test run in the Backyard Battleground, or dive into Solo Play to find favorites.  

Pro Tips:  
• Never stop moving! A moving target is harder to hit. Simple, right?
• Utilize each characters' special abilities to fully unlock their potential 
• Try Citron to stay alive longer. He has high health, shield ability, and can roll out of sticky situations using his ball form.

ID_M_PLAYLIST_GB_DESC: Plants and Zombies fight for control of the Gnome Bomb.  Its devastating power is perfect for destroying the opposing team's garden or graveyard.
ID_M_PLAYLIST_TURFTAKEOVER_DESC: The ultimate battle for Suburbia, featuring a mix of both Herbal Assault and Gardens & Graveyards modes where Plants and Zombies battle for control over an expanding map.
